note: Authored source of truth for the weekly edition prose. The build script reads this file, never overwrites a 'final' edition, and appends a 'draft' stub when a new Wednesday has no edition yet. Editions are bylined and finalised by a human.

editions.title: Asia now routes nearly half of everything
editions.dek: Europe authors the science. It runs almost none of the tokens.
body: This is the first edition of model adoption, and it opens on a number that should concentrate European minds. Across the last seven days of routed traffic on openrouter, models built in Asia carried 46 percent of all tokens, ahead of the United States on 40 percent. Models built in Europe carried 0.47 percent. Not four percent, not fourteen - under half of one.

The gap is not about capability and it is not about research. Europe authors a great deal of the science the whole field runs on. What it does not yet have is a developer whose models get reached for at scale. Mistral is the only European developer that routes here at all, and this week it sat outside the leaderboard entirely - below every Chinese lab you have heard of and several you have not.

The second story is speed. The top of the table is a lease, not a deed. tencent's hunyuan 3 preview took the number one routed slot off deepseek's v4 flash this week. xiaomi's mimo v2.5, ranked 42nd seven days ago, is now third. anthropic shipped claude 4.8 opus and it walked straight into the top fifteen. Hold a position here for a fortnight and you have done something rare.

So the watch point this instrument returns to every Wednesday is the distance between where models are written and where they are run. Right now that distance is enormous, and this week it widened in Asia's favour. The figures below reflect openrouter routing specifically - developer behaviour, cost-sensitive, and not the whole market - but it is the cleanest weekly read we have on what gets reached for, and reached for first.

call.this_week: Asia stays ahead of the US in routed share next Wednesday, and Europe stays below one percent.
call.grade_note: Both held. On 10 June Asia stayed comfortably ahead of the US in routed share, and Europe stayed below one percent. It has only widened since.

editions.title: Asia routes three tokens in five. The frontier moved east and stayed.
editions.dek: Five weeks ago this instrument opened on Asia at 46 percent of routed tokens. It is now 61 percent, and the six most-routed models are all built in China.
body: When model adoption launched in June, Asia carried 46 percent of the tokens routed through OpenRouter, and that already looked high. Five weeks on it is 60.6 percent, up almost five points in the last seven days alone, while the United States has slipped to 32.7 percent. The line did not wobble and revert. It bent once and kept bending.

The concentration is sharper than the regional split suggests. The six most-routed models this week, Tencent's hy3, Xiaomi's MiMo, two DeepSeek v4 variants, MiniMax M3 and Z-ai's GLM 5.2, are all Chinese-built, and most are open-weight. The first American model on the board, Anthropic's Claude 4.7 Opus, sits seventh at 4.3 percent. Europe remains a rounding error at half a percent, and drifted down again this week.

One caveat holds the whole read together: this is OpenRouter routing traffic, which is developer-skewed and cost-sensitive and excludes most direct enterprise API usage. It measures where builders reach first when they are paying by the token, not the whole market. But that is precisely the leading edge. For a European team choosing a default model on price and performance today, the honest answer is increasingly an Asian-built, open-weight one, and the trendline says that answer is hardening, not softening.

call.this_week: Asia holds above 55 percent of routed tokens next Wednesday, and the top five models remain Chinese-built.
